reviews.review_msg: In overall, I found him a good instructor. Some did not like his seriousness and marking style. I had no specific problem with his marking

now that I am out of school, on a job, I find his teaching quite useful in my job practices. He knew very well about what he was talking. Quite different from those teachers who know the theory, but have zero practical experience
Does not know how to teach. Probably the worst prof at SFU.
Ask a friend or family member to read you the notes.  It's just like going to class.

Best prof ever. Nice attitude towards students. interested in the subject based on his long industrial experience. Taught us real world examples. Exams are a bit hard but you will get good mark if you attend his lectures and work hard.

Very nice man but terrible teacher. Read out answers in lectures instead of explaining how to get the answers. Tutorials not much better. Read textbook carefully for good explanations. Huge assignments for 10% of mark overall. Unfair T/F on midterm-had to decifer his bad grammar before answering a question not related to the lecture/tutorial/hw
